“It is not illegal to visit the airport,” said the spokesman, Abdel-Basim Abdel-Husseini. “We have said that it is not illegal to visit the airport.”

The French government has also threatened to remove the airport from Paris, an official added.<|endoftext|>The U.S. Supreme Court on Tuesday issued a landmark ruling that gives the U.S. government permission to use a GPS tracking device to remotely change or track a person’s movements.

The ruling, which came in a decision that was based on the same court opinion, leaves the government with no option but to use a technology that allows them to change the person’s GPS location based on the GPS track record.
